assertion_results.text: A base error class (AppError or similar) exists with properties: code, message, statusCode, category, and cause
assertion_results.evidence: AppError class in src/errors/AppError.ts with code, message, statusCode, category, cause properties. Extends Error with proper prototype chain and Error.captureStackTrace.

assertion_results.text: At least 5 error subclasses are defined covering: validation (400), authentication (401), authorization (403), not-found (404), and conflict (409)
assertion_results.evidence: Found 7 subclasses: ValidationError (400), AuthenticationError (401), AuthorizationError (403), NotFoundError (404), ConflictError (409), RateLimitError (429), InternalError (500). Each in its own file under src/errors/.

assertion_results.text: Each error class has a stable string error code (e.g., ERR_NOT_FOUND_USER) not just an HTTP status number
assertion_results.evidence: All error classes use stable string codes: ERR_VALIDATION, ERR_VALIDATION_FIELD, ERR_AUTH_INVALID, ERR_AUTH_EXPIRED, ERR_FORBIDDEN, ERR_NOT_FOUND_USER, ERR_CONFLICT_EMAIL, ERR_RATE_LIMIT, ERR_INTERNAL

assertion_results.text: A centralized error middleware function handles all errors and produces a consistent JSON response format
assertion_results.evidence: errorHandler middleware in src/middleware/errorHandler.ts. Handles AppError instances with their properties and unknown errors with generic 500 response. Consistent { error: { code, message, details? }, requestId } format.

assertion_results.text: Error responses include a requestId or correlationId field for debugging
assertion_results.evidence: Middleware extracts x-request-id header with crypto.randomUUID() fallback. requestId included in every error response body and every structured log entry.

assertion_results.text: User-facing error responses do not contain stack traces, file paths, or internal implementation details
assertion_results.evidence: AppError responses expose only code, message, and safe details. Unknown errors return { code: 'ERR_INTERNAL', message: 'An unexpected error occurred' }. Stack traces logged internally only via structured logger.

assertion_results.text: Structured error logging includes at minimum: error code, message, stack trace, correlation ID, and request path
assertion_results.evidence: Structured JSON logger outputs: { code, message, category, correlationId, path, method, stack, cause, timestamp, service }. Uses winston with JSON transport.

assertion_results.text: The error response JSON schema is consistent across all endpoints with at least code and message fields
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 routes throw typed AppError subclasses. No inline res.status().json() error responses. Error middleware produces consistent schema for all error types.
